brief: Tuesday. The VP saw the eval thread. Legal responded to your email with 'Can we find time this week?' The demo is tomorrow. Nobody has confirmed the staging environment works.

modifier.title: Back-to-Back Tuesday
modifier.description: Your calendar is fully blocked until 4 PM. Every choice today costs 1 extra teamHealth.

scenario.title: The VP Is Live-Frustrated
scenario.flavor: 9:15 AM. The VP skipped the usual pleasantries.
scenario.description: The VP of Product messages you directly: 'I saw the eval thread. I need to understand the risk to Thursday's launch before I talk to the board. Can you join a call at 10?' It is 9:15. You have 45 minutes to prepare.

choices.text: Prep a crisp risk summary and join the call.
choices.consequence: The VP appreciates the clarity. They share the summary with the board. Your name is now associated with being calm under pressure.

choices.text: Join the call without prep. Speak from memory.
choices.consequence: You handle it. Mostly. The VP leaves the call with one more question than when they entered. That question is now a Slack thread.

choices.text: Reschedule to 2 PM. 'I want to give you accurate numbers.'
choices.consequence: The VP agrees. You now have 5 hours to get accurate numbers. This was the right call but it cost social capital.

randomEvent.title: Board Member CCed on the Thread
randomEvent.description: A board member was inadvertently copied on the eval concern thread. They have not responded. Their silence is heavy.

rareEvent.title: The VP Goes to Bat for You
rareEvent.description: The VP, impressed by your preparation, tells the board the team has a solid mitigation plan. You did not know they would do this. It helps enormously.

scenario.title: Legal Found Something
scenario.flavor: 11:45 AM. The calendar invite says 'Quick Legal Sync'. It is never quick.
scenario.description: Legal has flagged a data handling issue: user conversation logs are being retained longer than the DPA states. It affects 40,000 EU users. They need a response plan before the launch. 'This needs to be resolved before any public announcement of the AI feature.'

choices.text: Halt the launch. Fix the data pipeline first.
choices.consequence: Launch delayed. Legal is satisfied. Engineering scrambles. The board is informed. You sleep slightly better than you would have otherwise.

choices.text: Fix it in parallel — launch non-EU first.
choices.consequence: Smart segmentation. EU launch delayed 1 week. US launch proceeds. Legal rates this 'acceptable with conditions'.

choices.text: Accept the risk. Ship globally, fix post-launch.
choices.consequence: You ship. Legal documents their objection. The fix is 'in the next release'. Risk is now your middle name.

randomEvent.title: GDPR Inquiry Email Arrives
randomEvent.description: An automated inquiry from an EU data authority arrives. Probably routine. Legal says it's 'probably routine' with the energy of someone who is not certain.

rareEvent.title: Legal Counsel Writes You a Compliment
rareEvent.description: Your Legal contact sends a message: 'Your team's handling of this is genuinely impressive.' This has never happened before in recorded company history.

scenario.title: Demo Tomorrow, Build Today
scenario.flavor: 2:30 PM. The demo is in 18 hours. The feature is 65% done.
scenario.description: The quarterly exec demo is tomorrow at 10 AM. The feature is functional but rough. The happy path works. The unhappy path crashes. You have 18 hours, a tired team, and one engineer who hasn't committed code since Friday.

choices.text: Scope down the demo to the working 65%.
choices.consequence: The demo covers a smaller surface but everything you show works. Executives appreciate polish over breadth. This was the right call.

choices.text: Crunch tonight. Get to 90% by 9 AM.
choices.consequence: The team pushes hard. You hit 85%. The remaining 15% crashes when clicked. You will not click it in the demo. You have memorized the safe path.

choices.text: Demo from staging only. Document what's missing.
choices.consequence: Transparent and professional. Execs appreciate the honesty. One exec asks 'Why isn't it done?' You have a good answer prepared.

randomEvent.title: Staging Environment Goes Down at 3 PM
randomEvent.description: Infra Slack: 'staging is having issues, working on it.' It is not resolved by 5 PM. The demo environment is uncertain.

rareEvent.title: Engineer Heroics: 'I Fixed the Crash'
rareEvent.description: A senior engineer stays late voluntarily and patches the crash. Unprompted. The feature is now at 80%. Team morale spikes briefly.

scenario.title: Alert at 5:12 PM
scenario.flavor: 5:12 PM. PagerDuty. The demo is tomorrow at 10 AM.
scenario.description: An alert fires: API error rate elevated to 12%, primarily in the inference service. It's not catastrophic but it's visible to users. The on-call engineer is new. The VP has not seen this yet. The demo is tomorrow.

choices.text: Take the lead. Join the war room now.
choices.consequence: You coordinate the response. The issue is resolved in 90 minutes. The inference service is stable before midnight. The team is exhausted but the demo is safe.

choices.text: Delegate to the on-call engineer. Brief the VP proactively.
choices.consequence: The VP appreciates the heads-up. The on-call engineer resolves it independently. You discover they're capable. This was good for everyone.

choices.text: Monitor from the sidelines. It may self-resolve.
choices.consequence: It does not self-resolve. You are woken at 11 PM by a second alert. The on-call engineer is now also awake.

randomEvent.title: Users Notice the Status Page Is Outdated
randomEvent.description: Someone tweets that your status page shows 'all systems operational' while they're experiencing errors. The tweet gets 47 likes. Your social media manager sees it.

rareEvent.title: Root Cause Identified Instantly
rareEvent.description: The on-call engineer identifies the root cause in 8 minutes: a misconfigured timeout added in this morning's deploy. Fixed in 12 minutes. Total incident duration: 20 minutes. Team efficiency rating: high.

quests.title: Rebuild VP Trust
quests.description: Take at least 1 decisive action that directly addresses the VP's concerns today.

quests.title: Demo-Ready by 9 AM
quests.description: Make 2 decisions today that contribute to a stable, demonstrable product.

quests.title: Clear the Compliance Risk
quests.description: Address the legal data issue before it escalates to the board.
